item.title: DDA review is a right call says ACT NOW!
item.description: [Act Now PNG] There is clearly a huge transparency and accountability gap that still exists within DDAs. The call by the Constitutional Law Reform Commission to review DDA law is a right call.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: How a local fintech solved two ‘serious’ problems with land management
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Port Moresby firm NiuPay has found a way to turn Papua New Guinea's new strata title law into a working, digital reality. Its Digital Strata Title Platform was one of the winners at this year's Innovation PNG Awards, with founder James Inglis sharing how it works – and the problems it solves – in hi

item.title: Protecting Pacific Taro
item.description: [Education News PNG] USP Researcher Tackles Climate Challenges As the world marks World Rural Development Day on 6 July, attention turns to the vital role rural communities play The post Protecting Pacific Taro first appeared on Education News PNG .

item.title: PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery
item.description: [Inside PNG] NAQIA restricts Australian poultry imports after H5N1 bird flu found in WA wild birds. Free-range WA products suspended whilst barn-raised are safe. The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imp

item.title: PNG Leader Urges World Powers to Keep Pacific "Ocean of Peace" After Chinese Missile Test
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape has urged major military powers across the globe to honour the Pacific as a zone of peace, after China fired a long-range ballistic missile into open waters in the South Pacific region as part of a test launch, directly affecting waters close to Papua New Guinea and its n

item.title: Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea faces significant gold smuggling issues, undermining its economy and reducing revenue for essential services. Efforts are being made to enhance monitoring and disrupt smuggling networks for better resource management. The post Gold Smuggling in Papua New Guinea first appeared on PAW

item.title: The Coolest Vanity Apps for You and Your Girls
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG PM Urges World Powers to Keep Pacific Free From Military Weapons Testing
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has appealed to China and other military powers to stop using the Pacific Ocean for missile tests and weapons trials after a strategic ballistic missile was launched into international waters in the South Pacific. Describing the Pacific as a region that should be prot

item.title: Tonga Ratifies CTBT, Completing Treaty Universalisation in Pacific Region
item.description: [Post Courier] Tonga has ratified the Comprehensive Nuclear-Test-Ban Treaty (CTBT), becoming the 179th State to do so and completing universalisation of the Treaty across the Pacific region. For a part of the world deeply impacted by nuclear testing, it is a milestone long in the making. The post Tonga Ratifies CT

item.title: PNG Chiefs warn public against counterfeit merchandise
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PNG Chiefs are aware that unauthorised merchandise bearing a fraudulent version of the club’s logo is being produced, distributed and sold across Papua New Guinea, and are warning fans and the public not to purchase these counterfeit products. In a media statement released yesterday, the club wishes

item.title: Retired Judge Don Sawong appointed as Acting Commissioner for Watchdog ICAC
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The ICAC Appointments Committee has appointed retired judge Don Sawong as Acting Commissioner of the Independent Commission Against Corruption (ICAC). The appointment was formalized during a swearing-in and employment contract signing ceremony at Government House this morning. Related news: https://

item.title: Ol ami rausim planti bom long East New Britain provins
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol bom ekspet na sapot opisa bilong dispela wok i kam long Australia, PNG, New Zealand, Canada, France, Japan, UK na USA i bin stap insait long wanpela operesen we ol i rausim inap 2,200 bom bilong Wol Woa 2 long East New Britain provins long Jun dispela yia. Australia i go pas long dispela wok anin

item.title: Mining Project Sparks Fears of Environmental Disaster
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Major mining project that could discharge hundreds of millions of tonnes of waste into the sea off Papua New Guinea

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘We’re always looking for opportunities’: Air Niugini’s new CEO on his first items of business
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Alan Milne was appointed to his second term as CEO of Air Niugini in early 2026, following a previous stint in 2018-2020. In this interview with Business Advantage PNG , Milne speaks about changing market conditions and plans for more planes and new routes, as well as a pending new corporate strateg

item.title: USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World University Innovation category ranking
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has strengthened its standing among the world’s internationally recognized universities after climbing more than 400 places in the The post USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World Univ

item.title: Ten (10) Arrested Including Two (2) Chinese Nationals Over Alleged Murder Of Young PNG Man
item.description: [Inside PNG] Police in Port Moresby have arrested ten suspects over the death of a 20 year old man and the attempted murder of another victim. Among the arrests were four security guards from Fix Security Firm, four shop assistants and two Chinese expatriates from Cash and Carry Shopping Center at Gerehu’s Rainb

item.title: Newmont PNG expands trade training with national accreditation
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Newmont PNG has reached another milestone in workforce development after its Lihir Technical Training Centre secured national accreditation to conduct onsite Belt Splicing trade testing, giving more Papua New Guineans access to recognised trade qualifications without leaving the mine site. Newmont P

item.title: TVET and Universities in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's education system offers two main pathways: Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) focuses on practical skills for immediate employment, while universities emphasise theoretical knowledge and long-term career preparation. The post TVET and Universities in Papua New

item.title: PNG PM Marape Removes Minister Win Daki from Commerce Portfolio Pending SME Fund Probe
item.description: [PNG Facts] Prime Minister James Marape has stripped Minister Win Daki of the Commerce and Industry portfolio as the Government launches an independent investigation into allegations involving the administration of Papua New Guinea's Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) Fund. The move also requires Commerce and In

item.title: Six Manus Clans Renew Conservation Commitment to Protect the Manus Great Central Forest
item.description: [Post Courier] Six clans from Manus Province have reaffirmed their commitment to environmental protection by renewing and extending their conservation deed (CD) agreements, protecting more than 1,295 hectares of the globally significant Manus Great Central Forest, during a ceremony held at Lehewa village along the

item.title: Police bosses urged to provide strong leadership during the 2027 NGE
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI COMMISSIONER of Police David Manning has urged Provincial Police Commanders (PPCs), deputy commissioners, assistant commissioners, border commanders, and other senior police officers to bring their expertise, skills, knowledge, and leadership to ensure effective security for the 2027

item.title: God is not done with you, preacher Kalan tells Worshipers
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Christians were encouraged to remain faithful through life’s hardships with the assurance that God has a purpose for every challenge, as preacher Steward Kalan reminded worshippers that “God is not done with you.” Preaching last Sabbath from Jeremiah 29:11 at Sogavare Memorial SDA in Gerehu Stage 6

item.title: Mt. Bagana na El Niño givim hevi long Bogenvil
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Maunten paia long Mt. Bagana wantaim drai bilong El Nino i kamapim planti hevi long ol manmeri long Bogenvil. Ol i sot long gutpela wara na kaikai long ples we i stap klostu long we asis bilong maunten i pundaun.

item.description: [Act Now PNG] Constitutional Law Reform Commission (CLRC) will review the laws on DDA

Trump, his administration and the American people following celebrations marking 250 years of American independence on July 4. The Prime Minister described the milestone as a significant mo

item.title: 42,000 teaching positions vacancies nationwide for 2027 academic year: TSC
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Teaching Service Commission (TSC) has officially released Circular Instruction No. 8 of 2026, announcing exactly 42,000 teaching positions vacancies across the country for the 2027 academic year. TSC Acting Chairman Maini Mike Ugaia confirmed that this massive deployment consists of 25,147 base-

item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has assured Gulf Province and Papua LNG landowners they will receive greater long-term benefits from the multi-billion-Kina project, saying negotiations with developers are close to delivering a stronger deal for Papua New Guinea. item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: PNG and the Failure of EUDR Benchmarking: When “Low Risk” Ignores Reality
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Papua New Guinea’s designation as “low risk” in the EUDR may now be one of the clearest examples of the disconnect with reality.

The appointment follows weeks of speculation over the position and comes after

item.title: PM Marape, Kikori MP launch new Market, K315 million Development Package for Kikori
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape and local Kikori MP, Soroi Marepo Eoe launched more than K315 million in infrastructure projects and officially opened the new Ihu Market last Friday. The launch was described as a major investment boost for developments in Kikori District, Gulf province. Related news: h

item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. item.title: Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji
item.description: [Pawa TV] Fiji significantly leads in tourism development over Papua New Guinea, generating USD 2.5 billion compared to PNG's USD 182.94 million, highlighting the need for PNG to improve accessibility and marketing. item.description: [Post Courier] The Government’s Fuel Relief Assistance has been extended into its third month, maintaining domestic pump prices at March 2026 levels despite a significant drop in global oil prices to pre-war levels. Mr. Toka will lead the company’s government and institutional relations and stakeholder eng

item.title: Early Campaigning in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Concerns grow in Papua New Guinea over early campaigning, which breaches electoral laws. item.title: Manning clarifies Police’s role for 2027 NGE
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GINNAH MINI WITH the 2027 National General Election fast approaching, Commissioner of Police David Manning has assured Papua New Guineans that the Royal Papua New Guinea Constabulary (RPNGC) is committed to providing a safe and secure environment to ensure the elections are conducted peacefully.

item.title: PM Marape to reshuffle Cabinet this week
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister, James Marape has announced a Cabinet reshuffle later this week following a review of ministers’ performance, saying the changes are aimed at accelerating delivery of Government policies before the 2027 National General Election. PM Marape said the reshuffle, expected on Thursday or F

item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] A small poultry farm in Papua New Guinea’s Central Province has found a sustainable way of reducing its dependence on expensive stock feed. item.title: Cancer Treatment in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Cancer poses a significant health challenge in Papua New Guinea, exacerbated by late diagnoses, limited screening, and inadequate treatment facilities. item.description: [Post Courier] In a major move to institutionalize volunteer training, the National Volunteer Service (NVS) has concluded feasibility studies and entered high-level negotiations to establish permanent Regional Volunteer Academies across all four regions. item.title: SP Brewery backs Mask Festival with K10,000
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] SOUTH Pacific Brewery has committed K10 000 to the committees of the upcoming 29th National Mask and Warwagira Festival that will be staged at Kalabond Sports Field in Kokopo,East New Britain Province. This reaffirmed it’s commitment to celebrating and preserving Papua New Guinea’s rich cultural her

item.title: Westpac PNG forecasts solid growth as PNG Economy remains resilient
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Papua New Guinea’s economy has remained resilient despite global uncertainty and rising energy prices, according to a commercial bank report. Westpac PNG bank has forecasted real economic growth of 4.6 per cent this year, driven by strong resource exports and expanding non-resource sectors. Related

item.title: ACT NOW! files second legal challenge against the PNG Forest Authority

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘The skills that matter are changing fast’: Q&A with Ben Hamer, keynote speaker at 2026 PNG Investment Conference
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Dr Ben Hamer, an international expert on the future of work, shares his views on which skills will be most in demand in the economies of the future – and where Papua New Guinea fits into the changing nature of work. item.title: PNG Faces High Likelihood of Extraordinary El Niño and Prolonged Drought
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG braces for an extraordinary dry season with looming water shortages and crop stress. Districts ordered to map local water sources. item.title: SP PNG Hunters at the Crossroads as Last-Place Finish Raises Tough Questions Over Direction
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Commentary by George Lemko The PNG SP Hunters have reached a point where difficult questions can no longer be ignored. The latest Hostplus Cup ladder paints a worrying picture, with the Hunters sitting at the bottom of the 15-team competition after another disappointing campaign. While rival clubs c

item.title: The Story of the Meri Blouse
item.description: [Pawa TV] A Symbol of PNG Identity. The meri blouse, locally also called kolos in Tok Pisin, is one of the most recognisable garments associated with women in Papua New Guinea (PNG). Featuring a loose, comfortable fit often crafted from colourful fabric with bold motifs, it serves not just as clothing but as

item.title: Different New Year Celebrations Around the World
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG PM Marape Confirms Suwae as Acting PNG Chief Ombudsman
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG PM Marape Confirms Suwae as Acting PNG Chief Ombudsman PNG Prime Minister James Marape has confirmed the appointment of Tabitha Suwae as Acting Chief Ombudsman following the completion of Richard Pagen's six-year term, with the Government beginning the process of recruiting a permanent head for

item.title: Frontline Deployments as NVS Sends Health and Agri Volunteers to Remote Outposts
item.description: [Post Courier] Crucial sectors in rural Papua New Guinea are receiving immediate relief as the National Volunteer Service (NVS) rolls out targeted deployments, dominated heavily by frontline healthcare and agricultural specialists. item.title: Marape departs for high-level PNG–Australia talks in Brisbane
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Prime Minister James Marape is travelling to Brisbane, Australia today to lead a series of high-level bilateral, regional and people-to-people engagements aimed at further strengthening Papua New Guinea’s strategic partnership with Australia. The official visit, from 7–9 July, will culminate in the

item.title: Daki stripped off Commerce Portfolio as PM Marape orders probe into K200m SME Fund
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Minister for Commerce and Industry Win Daki has been stripped of his ministerial portfolio pending an independent investigation into the Government’s K200 million Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) Fund, with Finance Minister Thomas Opa taking over the ministry in addition to his existing responsibil

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Iconic brewer looking to make a social impact in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] SP Brewery’s beers have been sold in PNG since 1952, almost a quarter-century before independence. Now the company is looking to invest in social change alongside business growth opportunities, its Managing Director Ed Weggemans tells Business Advantage PNG . item.title: Chief Inspector Kelly Sombe Appointed New Southern Highlands Provincial Police Commander
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Chief Inspector Kelly Sombe has officially been appointed as the new Provincial Police Commander (PPC) for Southern Highlands Province, effective immediately. The appointment was confirmed in an official police minute dated 30 June 2026, signed by Deputy Commissioner of Police and Chief of Regional

item.title: PM Marape arrives in Ihu to launch K316 million infrastructure programme
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister James Marape this morning arrived in Ihu, Kikori District, to launch more than K316 million worth of infrastructure and community projects aimed at improving transport, markets and public services across East and West Ihu Local Level Governments. item.description: [Inside PNG] Nine Chinese nationals arrested in PNG's biggest gold-smuggling bust. 14.5kg of gold sand found on flight PX8. Strict bail granted. item.title: PNG Defence Force Personnel Complete Trade Training Under Australia Partnership

item.title: Community effort gives Gumose students a better place to learn
item.description: [Post Courier] Children at Gumose Community School in Raicoast District, Madang will have a better place to learn after the people of Gumbarambi Village built a new administration building and library through their own efforts. item.title: PM Marape to reshuffle Cabinet this week to ensure delivery of Government policy commitments
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] Prime Minister James Marape has announced that he will undertake a Cabinet reshuffle and portfolio restructuring later this week as part of an effort to make sure Government delivers on its commitments to the people of Papua New Guinea. The Prime Minister said the reshuffle, expected on Thursday or

item.title: Office of Censorship destroys 645 prohibited items
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The Office of Censorship has destroyed 645 prohibited items confiscated during enforcement operations across Papua New Guinea in 2025, reinforcing its commitment to enforcing censorship laws and protecting the public from illegal imports. item.title: PNG Democracy Can Recover Under New Electoral Commissioner, Says Ipatas
item.description: [PNG Facts] Enga Governor Chief Sir Peter Ipatas Enga Governor Chief Sir Peter Ipatas says Papua New Guinea has endured too many disappointing elections, but believes the country has an opportunity to restore public confidence following the appointment of Dr Nicole Haley as Electoral Commissioner. item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: PNG Trade Union Calls for Clarity on Foreign Appointments
